name: Corsica (Corse / Corsica)
summary: Corsica (Corse in French / Corsica in Corsican) is a Mediterranean civil-law sui generis collectivité territoriale of the French Republic — structurally distinctive globally as the only French sui generis territorial collectivity (Collectivité de Corse / Collectività di Corsica) operating outside both the standard French DROM/COM frameworks and the standard French metropolitan region framework, established by the merger of Haute-Corse and Corse-du-Sud departments and the former Collectivité Territoriale de Corse into a single Collectivité de Corse on 1 January 2018 via the Loi NOTRe 2015 and Ordonnance 2016-1561, and as the only metropolitan French territory with a distinctive arrêtés Miot 1801 fiscal-succession framework (preserving the historical Genoese Statutes of 1571 framework, abolished only in 2018 by Loi 2017-285 — most recent abolition of a 19th-century special fiscal framework in metropolitan France). Corsica has a distinct Statut of 2002 (Statut de la Collectivité de Corse, Loi 2002-92) granting limited legislative-experimentation powers under Article 72 of the French Constitution. The Corsican National Liberation Front (FLNC) armed independence-movement framework operated from 1976-2014; FLNC dissolved 25 June 2014. Family-law framework operates under the French Civil Code (Code civil 1804) applied as in metropolitan France via Bastia/Ajaccio judicial framework. Parental authority (autorité parentale) and child custody operate under Civil Code Articles 371-387. The Court of Appeal of Bastia is the apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Corsica; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the French Court of Cassation in Paris. Corsica is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. France is a Hague Convention 1980 party (acceded 1 December 1983) — Corsica Hague applicability via French metropolitan extension.

statutory_framework.citation: Loi NOTRe 2015 + Ordonnance 2016-1561 (Collectivité de Corse establishment)
statutory_framework.title: Collectivité de Corse establishment 2018
statutory_framework.relevance: French Federal Law and Ordonnance establishing Collectivité de Corse effective 1 January 2018 via merger of Haute-Corse and Corse-du-Sud departments and former Collectivité Territoriale de Corse.

statutory_framework.citation: Statut de la Collectivité de Corse 2002 (Loi 2002-92)
statutory_framework.title: Corsica Statut 2002
statutory_framework.relevance: French Federal Law of 22 January 2002 establishing Corsica's distinctive Statut granting limited legislative-experimentation powers under Article 72 of the French Constitution.

statutory_framework.title: Loi 2017-285
statutory_framework.relevance: French Law of 6 March 2017 abolishing the arrêtés Miot fiscal-succession framework (preserved from Genoese Statutes 1571) effective 2018 — most recent abolition of a 19th-century special fiscal framework in metropolitan France.

statutory_framework.citation: French Civil Code arts. 371-387 (applicable in Corsica)
statutory_framework.title: French Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: French Civil Code applied directly in Corsica as in metropolitan France. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale and child custody.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Bastia (Cour d'appel de Bastia)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Corsica.

apex_courts.name: French Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Corsica.

professional_regulators.name: French Republic professional framework (applicable in Corsica)
professional_regulators.role: French professional regulatory framework for health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology applicable in Corsica.

anonymisation_convention: Corsican family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Corsican Republic + Paoli Constitution
key_developments.description: Corsican Republic founded by Pasquale Paoli 1755 following 1729+ Corsican Revolution against Genoese rule — adopted Paoli Constitution 1755 (one of first modern constitutions worldwide + influenced subsequent American Revolutionary thought). Pivotal pre-modern foundation framework predating subsequent 1768 French annexation.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Versailles + French annexation
key_developments.description: Treaty of Versailles of 15 May 1768 transferring Corsica from Republic of Genoa to France — formal French annexation following 1729-1768 Corsican Revolution + 1769 Battle of Ponte Novu French victory definitively ending Paoli Corsican Republic. Foundation framework for subsequent ~256-year French sovereignty over Corsica.

key_developments.title: Arrêtés Miot fiscal-succession framework
key_developments.description: Arrêtés Miot of 21 prairial an IX (10 June 1801) by André-François Miot establishing distinctive Corsican fiscal-succession framework preserving Genoese Statutes of 1571 — abolished only 2018 by Loi 2017-285 — structurally distinctive globally most recent abolition of 19th-century special fiscal framework in metropolitan France (~217-year preservation period).

key_developments.title: FLNC formation
key_developments.description: Corsican National Liberation Front (Fronte di Liberazione Naziunale Corsu / FLNC) formed 4-5 May 1976 — beginning of Corsican armed independence-movement framework + structurally distinctive globally only major contemporary metropolitan-European armed independence movement subject to formal unilateral dissolution.

key_developments.title: Statut Joxe Defferre — first Statut
key_developments.description: Loi 82-214 of 2 March 1982 (Defferre laws) establishing first Corsican Statut granting limited administrative autonomy — pivotal early-1980s decentralisation framework foundation for subsequent 2002 Statut de la Collectivité de Corse framework.

key_developments.title: Statut Joxe + 'peuple corse' rejection
key_developments.description: Loi 91-428 of 13 May 1991 (Statut Joxe) — Constitutional Council Decision 91-290 DC of 9 May 1991 explicitly rejected reference to 'peuple corse' (Corsican people) component within Article 1 — pivotal constitutional clarification rejecting subnational-ethnic-people framework + foundation for subsequent constitutional debates.

key_developments.title: Statut de la Collectivité de Corse
key_developments.description: Loi 2002-92 of 22 January 2002 (Statut Jospin) establishing Corsica's distinctive Statut granting limited legislative-experimentation powers under Article 72 of the French Constitution — structurally distinctive globally only French metropolitan territory with Article 72 legislative-experimentation framework.

key_developments.title: FLNC dissolution
key_developments.description: FLNC announced unilateral and definitive end to armed action on 25 June 2014 — ending 38-year armed independence-movement framework + pivotal modern resolution framework for Corsican armed-independence context. First major contemporary metropolitan-European armed independence movement subject to formal unilateral dissolution.

key_developments.title: Collectivité de Corse + arrêtés Miot abolition
key_developments.description: Collectivité de Corse effective 1 January 2018 via merger of Haute-Corse and Corse-du-Sud departments and former Collectivité Territoriale de Corse via Loi NOTRe 2015 + Ordonnance 2016-1561 + arrêtés Miot fiscal-succession framework abolition 2018 via Loi 2017-285 (~217-year arrêtés Miot preservation framework definitively ended).

key_developments.title: Macron Beauvau-process launch + Assemblée-de-Corse + Défenseur-des-droits framework
key_developments.description: President Macron launched Beauvau Corsican autonomy process September 2023 with Assemblée de Corse institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within French Civil Code Articles 371-387 framework applied via Bastia/Ajaccio judicial framework. Défenseur des droits institutional positions affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Continuing Collectivité de Corse framework + post-FLNC-dissolution autonomy framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Mediterranean French-sui-generis-collectivité institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Beauvau autonomy negotiations + constitutional Article 74 framework
key_developments.description: Continuing Beauvau Corsican autonomy negotiations 2024 — March 2024 draft constitutional Article 74 framework introducing Corsican constitutional autonomy under proposed constitutional amendment framework + continuing Collectivité de Corse + continuing Corsican-French language framework + continuing post-FLNC-dissolution autonomy framework + 256 years since 1768 French annexation.

structural_findings: Corsica operates a French-civil-law framework applied as in metropolitan France — places Corsica in the French metropolitan civil-law cluster with sui generis administrative framework.
Only French sui generis territorial collectivity operating outside both DROM/COM and standard metropolitan region frameworks is structurally distinctive globally.
Collectivité de Corse 2018 merger establishment is structurally distinctive within French administrative cluster.

Statut de la Collectivité de Corse 2002 limited legislative-experimentation powers under Article 72 is structurally distinctive — only French metropolitan territory with such legislative-experimentation framework.

Arrêtés Miot 1801 fiscal-succession framework (preserved from Genoese Statutes 1571) abolished only 2018 is structurally distinctive globally — most recent abolition of a 19th-century special fiscal framework in metropolitan France, ~217-year preservation period.

FLNC 1976-2014 armed independence-movement framework is structurally distinctive globally — only major contemporary metropolitan-European armed independence movement subject to formal unilateral dissolution.
French Hague Convention 1980 accession 1983 + Corsican civil-law framework intersection is structurally distinctive.
editorial_notes: Corsica jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — civil-law Mediterranean French sui generis Collectivité de Corse (1755 Corsican Republic Paoli Constitution + 1768 Treaty of Versailles French annexation + 1801 arrêtés Miot + 1976 FLNC formation + 1982 Statut Defferre + 1991 Statut Joxe peuple corse rejection + 2002 Statut de la Collectivité de Corse Article 72 + 2014 FLNC dissolution + 2018 Collectivité de Corse + arrêtés Miot abolition + 2024 Beauvau autonomy negotiations constitutional Article 74 framework). Only French sui generis territorial collectivity outside DROM/COM and standard metropolitan region frameworks globally + only French metropolitan territory with Article 72 legislative-experimentation framework + most recent abolition of 19th-century special fiscal framework in metropolitan France (~217-year arrêtés Miot preservation) + only major contemporary metropolitan-European armed independence movement subject to formal unilateral dissolution + 1755 Paoli Constitution one of first modern constitutions worldwide.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Mediterranean + civil-law + French-sui-generis-collectivity cluster + outside-DROM-COM-and-metropolitan-region-frameworks-globally-distinctive + Statut-de-la-Collectivité-de-Corse-2002-legislative-experimentation + arrêtés-Miot-1801-fiscal-succession-217-year-preservation + FLNC-1976-2014-armed-independence-movement-formal-dissolution + Treaty-of-Versailles-1768-Genoese-to-French-transfer clusters within the corpus.
